title: Dr. rer. nat. in Theoretical Physics (Material Science)

content.text:
Georg-August-Universität Göttingen (May 2018 - Oct 2021)
Grade: magna cum laude

content.html:
Atomic-Scale Friction and Energy Dissipation

My PhD research developed a molecular-dynamics framework to investigate how dry sliding interfaces dissipate energy through phonon modes. By studying a kicked atomic slider on an incommensurate substrate, I analyzed how translational kinetic energy evolves into vibrational motion and how this process governs friction at the microscopic level. Using classical MD and eigenmode analysis, the work provides a mode-resolved understanding of energy flow, thermalization, and nonlinear dynamics in low-dimensional systems.

items: Anharmonicity as the driver of friction: demonstrated how intrinsic and extrinsic anharmonicity (bond interactions, defects, boundary effects) controls mode coupling, resonance behavior, and the rate of thermalization.

Boundary-condition effects: showed that free-end sliders introduce additional dissipation channels (rotation, torsion, breathing modes), while rigid or periodic boundaries suppress these pathways and alter friction.
Defects and substrate geometry: identified how defects enhance pinning and friction, and how substrate periodicity determines resonance velocities and activation of specific vibrational modes.

content.html: Building on these results, the thesis provides a coherent physical picture of how microscopic structural features shape macroscopic friction. By comparing defect-free, defect-rich, and edge-modified systems, the work connects tribology with nonlinear dynamics and phonon transport. The findings form a foundation for extending such models to more realistic three-dimensional interfaces and for understanding friction in complex, disordered materials.

items.alt: The figure shows six of the most active vibrational modes of the harmonic obc (Open Boundary Conditions) slider. The solid red circles show the particles in the obc slider, and the arrows indicate the amplitude and direction of the vibrations.
Schematic view of ions trapped in a typical potential surface with a depth of U<u>0.

items.label: Friction on incommensurate substrates: Role of anharmonicity and defects (PRE)
